2606.03695.title: Don't Forget Your Embeddings: Robust Knowledge Erasure via Precise Editing of Embeddings
2606.03695.paper_summary: As language models are increasingly deployed in real-world applications, the ability to erase specific knowledge from them becomes critical for safety and compliance. Prominent methods seek persistent removal by updating the model's parameters, yet the target knowledge often can be recovered through adversarial prompting or relearning. In this work, we hypothesize this limitation stems in part from existing methods overlooking the embedding layer. To address this, we introduce EMBedding ERasure (EMBER), a plug-n-play erasure module that leverages Sparse Matrix Factorization for precise erasure of concept-related features from token embeddings. Through comprehensive evaluations across diverse concepts on Gemma-2-2B-it and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, we find that augmenting existing methods with EMBER consistently improves erasure efficacy and specificity across task formats, with minimal coherence loss. Moreover, it dramatically improves robustness to relearning, reducing regained accuracy by up to 50%, limiting it to 35% on Llama compared to 70%-76% for prior methods. Further analysis shows that the coherence cost is localized, affecting only a small set of concept-exclusive tokens. Our work establishes that precise embedding-level intervention is necessary for robust concept erasure, and demonstrates that existing methods can benefit from such augmentation.
2606.03695.paper_summary_zh: 隨著語言模型在現實應用中的日益普及，從中刪除特定知識的能力對於安全性和合規性變得至關重要。顯著的方法通過更新模型的參數來尋求持久的刪除，然而目標知識通常可以通過對抗性提示或重新學習來恢復。在這項工作中，我們假設這一限制部分源於現有方法忽略了嵌入層。為了解決這個問題，我們介紹了EMBedding ERasure（EMBER），這是一個即插即用的刪除模塊，利用稀疏矩陣分解精確刪除與概念相關的特徵從標記嵌入中。通過對Gemma-2-2B-it和Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct上多樣化概念的全面評估，我們發現將現有方法與EMBER相結合，始終能提高刪除的有效性和特異性，並且幾乎不會損失連貫性。此外，它顯著提高了對重新學習的穩健性，將恢復的準確率降低了多達50%，在Llama上限制在35%，而之前的方法則為70%-76%。進一步分析顯示，連貫性成本是局部的，只影響一小部分概念專屬的標記。我們的工作確立了精確的嵌入層干預對於穩健的概念刪除是必要的，並展示了現有方法可以從這種增強中受益。
2606.03695.author: Clara Haya Suslik et.al.
2606.03695.authors: Clara Haya Suslik, Or Shafran, Mor Geva
